why_tempus: Dr. Webb is looking for blood-based testing options when tissue is insufficient. Tempus offers liquid-biopsy panels that provide genomic profiling and an automatic conversion option from tissue testing.
supporting_crm_excerpt: He wants to avoid repeat biopsies but asked whether a blood-based option covers enough genes to be useful.

product_snapshot.display_text: xF is a 105-gene liquid-biopsy ctDNA panel that detects SNVs, INDELs, copy-number gains, and gene rearrangements.
xF+ is a 523-gene liquid-biopsy panel covering SNVs, INDELs, copy-number gains, and gene rearrangements.
Tempus describes an automatic conversion option from xT CDx to xF or xF+ when tumor tissue is insufficient.

objection_response.text: Tempus offers two liquid-biopsy panels to address your concern regarding panel breadth. The xF panel is a 105-gene liquid-biopsy ctDNA panel that detects SNVs, INDELs, copy-number gains, and gene rearrangements. For cases requiring additional coverage, the xF+ panel is a 523-gene liquid-biopsy panel covering SNVs, INDELs, copy-number gains, and gene rearrangements. Both options are available through an automatic conversion process when tumor tissue is insufficient.

meeting_script.text: Dr. Webb, I understand you are looking for blood-based testing options to avoid repeat biopsies when tissue is insufficient. Tempus offers the xF panel, which is a 105-gene liquid-biopsy ctDNA panel, and the xF+ panel, which is a 523-gene liquid-biopsy panel. We also provide an automatic conversion option from xT CDx to xF or xF+ when tumor tissue is insufficient. Would you like to review the specific gene coverage for these two liquid-biopsy panels today?
